description: REQ-IPC-009 says `DtoBlock` serializes as `{ id, type, content }` with `type` values mirroring TS BlockType (9 literals: `paragraph | heading-1..3 | bullet | numbered | code | quote | divider`). The Rust shape is `block_type: String` serialized under JSON key `type`. Nothing in Sprint 8 mandates ENUM validation on the Rust side: an upstream caller (or future deserializer) could place an arbitrary string in `block_type` (e.g., `"hedaing-1"` typo), which would silently cross the wire as `"type": "hedaing-1"`, reach the TS reducer, and land on `EditorViewState.blocks[].type: BlockType` — but `BlockType` is a literal union, so the JS object would be structurally typed correctly even though the value is invalid. There is NO compile-time or runtime validation closing this loophole. PROP-IPC-009 does not cover string validity; PROP-IPC-011's TS narrowing is on `status`, not `blocks[].type`.

fileEvidence.snippet: REQ-IPC-009 — DtoBlock shall serialize as { "id": string, "type": string, "content": string }. The Rust block_type: String field shall serialize under the JSON key type. Permitted type values mirror the TS BlockType literal union (paragraph | heading-1 | heading-2 | heading-3 | bullet | numbered | code | quote | divider).

recommendation: Replace `block_type: String` with a Rust enum `BlockTypeDto` carrying the 9 variants `Paragraph | Heading1 | Heading2 | Heading3 | Bullet | Numbered | Code | Quote | Divider` with `#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]` (and `#[serde(rename = "heading-1")]` etc. for the heading numerics). This shifts validation to compile-time on the Rust side. Add a PROP-IPC for round-trip: `serde_json::from_str::<DtoBlock>("\"hedaing-1\"")` shall return `Err`. Without an enum the contract is unenforceable from Rust.
